name: Browser Tools
description: Control a real browser: navigate, click, fill forms, take screenshots, read page content, upload files, save PDFs, drag and drop, and more
systemPrompt:
When the user needs to interact with a web page, scrape content, take screenshots, fill forms, upload files, save PDFs, drag and drop, or automate browser tasks, use the Browser Tools extension. Each tool wraps the agent-browser CLI.

Setup: If agent-browser is not installed, call `browser_setup` to install it and download Chrome for Testing automatically. This is a one-time setup. If `browser_check` shows agent-browser is not available, always suggest calling `browser_setup` first.

Workflow: Start with `browser_check` to verify the environment. Then `browser_navigate` to open a URL, `browser_snapshot` to get the accessibility tree with element refs, and `browser_click` / `browser_fill` / `browser_select` / `browser_set_checkbox` to interact. Use `browser_screenshot` to visually verify state and `browser_read` to extract readable content. Clean up with `browser_close` when done.

Visible browser: If the user wants to see the agent's browser operations in real time, first check if Chrome is installed: on macOS check `/Applications/Google Chrome.app`, on Linux check `which google-chrome`. If Chrome is not found, tell the user to install it first. If Chrome is available, launch it yourself using Bash: `open -a "Google Chrome" --args --remote-debugging-port=9222` on macOS, or the equivalent for the platform. Wait a few seconds for Chrome to start, then call `browser_connect port=9222` to connect. All subsequent operations will happen in the visible window. Always prefer this when the user asks to 'watch', 'see', or 'visually verify' the automation. Do NOT ask the user to launch Chrome manually.

setup-agent-browser.title: Set up Agent Browser
setup-agent-browser.description: Check whether agent-browser is installed and install it if needed.
setup-agent-browser.prompt: Check if agent-browser is installed using /browser_check. If not installed, call /browser_setup to install the agent-browser CLI and download Chrome for browser automation.

scrape-webpage.title: Scrape a webpage
scrape-webpage.description: Navigate to a URL and extract its readable content.
scrape-webpage.prompt: /browser_navigate open https://example.com and /browser_read get the page content
